Off Calendar Oct-09-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Michael E. Sandgren of DENTONS US LLP (415-267-4130), appearing for defendant and moving party Metalclad Insulation LLC.
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and opposing parties Beverly Ann Huber, AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO AND AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR OF BRUCE HUBER, DECEASED; Shelley Blowers, Paula Cronin, and Debbie Vandenburg, as WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR OF BRUCE HUBER, DECEASED.

RULING: DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Metalclad Insulation LLC's Motion for Summary Adjudication of Issues is DENIED. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826.) Plaintiff's responses to Defendant's special interrogatories preclude Defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong under Aguilar. The declaration of Donald Trueblood is insufficient to establish that no reasonable jury could find Plaintiff's evidence to constitute clear and convincing proof of malice, fraud or oppression. (Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc., (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48, 60-61.) The Court did not consider the deposition testimony of Wayne May or Donald Trueblood filed concurrently with Defendant's Reply. (Huynh Decl. Exs. N-O; San Diego Watercrafts, Inc. v. Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. (2002) 102 Cal.App.4th 308, 316.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Jennifer Roux of US Legal Support, CSR #11033, jroux@aikenwelch.com (Tel: 415-362-4346). (503/CML)
